questions.stem: Under ASC 250, a change in accounting principle (e.g., from FIFO to weighted average) is generally accounted for by:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospective application only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retrospective application to all prior periods presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: A cumulative effect adjustment to current-period income

choices.label: D
choices.text: A prior period adjustment charged to additional paid-in capital

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-5 requires that a change in accounting principle be reported through retrospective application. The entity adjusts the carrying amounts of assets and liabilities at the beginning of the first period presented, with a corresponding offset to the opening balance of retained earnings. All prior periods presented are restated as if the new principle had always been used.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-5は、会計方針の変更を遡及適用により報告することを要求しています。企業は表示される最初の期間の期首における資産・負債の帳簿価額を調整し、対応する金額を利益剰余金の期首残高に相殺します。表示される全ての過去の期間は、新しい方針が常に使用されていたかのように修正再表示されます。

questions.stem: A company revises the estimated useful life of equipment from 10 years to 7 years after using it for 4 years. This change is accounted for:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retrospectively, restating prior periods

choices.label: B
choices.text: Prospectively, with the new depreciation rate applied to the remaining book value over the remaining useful life

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an error correction

choices.label: D
choices.text: By recording a cumulative adjustment to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-17, a change in accounting estimate is accounted for prospectively. The remaining depreciable amount (book value less salvage) is depreciated over the new remaining useful life (7 - 4 = 3 years). No restatement of prior periods is made because estimates are inherently subject to revision.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-17に基づき、会計上の見積りの変更は将来に向かって（プロスペクティブに）処理されます。残存償却可能額（帳簿価額から残存価額を控除）は、新しい残存耐用年数（7 - 4 = 3年）にわたって減価償却されます。見積りは本質的に修正される性質のものであるため、過去の期間の修正再表示は行いません。

questions.stem: An entity discovers that depreciation expense was understated by $50,000 in a prior year. This is a material error. How should it be corrected?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record additional depreciation in the current year

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate the prior period financial statements (prior period adjustment to retained earnings)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Disclose the error in notes only

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record a cumulative effect adjustment in current year income

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-23 requires that material errors in previously issued financial statements be corrected by restating the prior period financial statements. The correction is reported as a prior period adjustment to the opening balance of retained earnings in the earliest period presented. Comparative financial statements are revised.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-23は、過去に発行された財務諸表における重要な誤謬を、過去の期間の財務諸表の修正再表示により訂正することを要求しています。訂正は、表示される最も早い期間の利益剰余金の期首残高に対する前期修正として報告されます。比較財務諸表も修正されます。

questions.stem: A change in reporting entity (such as presenting consolidated statements for the first time) requires:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospective treatment only

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retrospective application—all prior periods must be restated to show the new reporting entity

choices.label: C
choices.text: A cumulative effect adjustment

choices.label: D
choices.text: No accounting change; disclosure only

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-21 requires that a change in the reporting entity be applied retrospectively. Financial statements of all prior periods presented must be restated to reflect the financial statements of the new reporting entity. This ensures comparability across periods presented.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-21は、報告主体の変更を遡及適用することを要求しています。表示される全ての過去の期間の財務諸表は、新しい報告主体の財務諸表を反映するよう修正再表示しなければなりません。これにより表示される期間間の比較可能性が確保されます。

questions.stem: Under ASC 250, when is it acceptable to NOT apply retrospective treatment for a change in accounting principle?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the change results in a decrease in net income

choices.label: B
choices.text: When retrospective application is impracticable

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the change involves inventory methods

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the auditors recommend against it

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-7 provides an impracticability exception. Retrospective application is impracticable when: (a) the effects cannot be determined despite reasonable effort, (b) the entity cannot determine assumptions about management's intent, or (c) significant estimates are required that cannot be objectively verified. In such cases, the change is applied prospectively from the earliest practicable date.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-7は実務上の不可能性の例外を規定しています。遡及適用が実務上不可能となるのは：(a) 合理的な努力にもかかわらず影響額を決定できない場合、(b) 経営者の意図に関する仮定を決定できない場合、または(c) 客観的に検証できない重要な見積りが必要な場合です。このような場合、変更は実務上可能な最も早い日から将来に向かって適用されます。

questions.stem: A change from straight-line to double-declining balance depreciation is treated as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: A change in accounting principle requiring retrospective application

choices.label: B
choices.text: A change in accounting estimate effected by a change in accounting principle (prospective)

choices.label: C
choices.text: An error correction

choices.label: D
choices.text: A change in reporting entity

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-45-18 states that a change in depreciation method is treated as a change in estimate effected by a change in accounting principle. It is accounted for prospectively (like a change in estimate) because depreciation methods are a means of recognizing asset cost over its useful life—inseparable from the estimate of useful life and residual value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-18は、減価償却方法の変更は、会計方針の変更を伴う会計上の見積りの変更として取り扱うと規定しています。減価償却方法は耐用年数にわたって資産原価を認識する手段であり、耐用年数や残存価額の見積りと不可分であるため、将来に向かって（見積りの変更と同様に）処理されます。

questions.stem: When a change in accounting principle is made, which of the following disclosures is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the name of the new accounting principle

choices.label: B
choices.text: The nature of and reason for the change, the method of applying the change, and the effect on income from continuing operations and net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: A statement that the auditor approved the change

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclosure is not required if the change increases net income

questions.explanation: ASC 250-10-50-1 requires disclosure of: the nature and reason for the change (including why the new principle is preferable), the method of applying the change (retrospective or prospective if impracticable), the effect on income from continuing operations, net income, EPS, and any other affected line items for all periods presented.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-50-1は以下の開示を要求しています：変更の性質と理由（新しい方針が望ましい理由を含む）、変更の適用方法（遡及適用、または実務上不可能な場合は将来適用）、継続事業からの利益、当期純利益、1株当たり利益（EPS）、および表示される全ての期間のその他の影響を受ける項目への影響。

questions.stem: An error in a previously issued interim financial statement of the current fiscal year is discovered in a subsequent interim period. How should it be corrected?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Correct it in the current interim period by including the cumulative effect

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restate the previously issued interim period if it will be reissued

choices.label: C
choices.text: Wait until year-end to correct

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose in notes only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250-10-45-25, if comparative interim financial statements are being presented, the prior interim period's financial statements should be restated to correct the error. If prior interim statements will not be reissued, the correction is included in the determination of net income for the current interim period with disclosure.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250-10-45-25に基づき、比較中間財務諸表が表示される場合、誤謬を訂正するために過去の中間期間の財務諸表を修正再表示すべきです。過去の中間財務諸表が再発行されない場合、訂正は開示を伴って当期の中間期間の当期純利益の算定に含められます。

questions.stem: When a company changes from LIFO to FIFO, the retrospective application requirement means:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Inventory is restated to FIFO for all periods presented and opening retained earnings is adjusted

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the current year uses FIFO; prior years remain as originally reported

choices.label: C
choices.text: A cumulative catch-up is recorded in the current year's income statement

choices.label: D
choices.text: The change is treated prospectively because LIFO data from prior years is unavailable

questions.explanation: A change from LIFO to FIFO is a change in accounting principle accounted for retrospectively under ASC 250-10-45-5. All prior periods presented must be restated as if FIFO had always been used. Opening retained earnings of the earliest period presented is adjusted for the cumulative effect. If retrospective application is impracticable, the earliest practicable date is used.
questions.explanationJa: LIFOからFIFOへの変更は、ASC 250-10-45-5に基づき遡及適用される会計方針の変更です。表示される全ての過去の期間は、FIFOが常に使用されていたかのように修正再表示されなければなりません。表示される最も早い期間の利益剰余金の期首残高が累積的影響額について調整されます。遡及適用が実務上不可能な場合は、実務上可能な最も早い日が使用されます。

questions.stem: Ending inventory in Year 1 was overstated by $20,000. If this error is not corrected, what is the effect on Year 2's net income (assuming beginning inventory Year 2 is ending inventory Year 1)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Year 2 net income is understated by $20,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Year 2 net income is overstated by $20,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Year 2 net income is not affected

choices.label: D
choices.text: Year 2 net income is understated by $40,000

questions.explanation: An overstatement of ending inventory in Year 1 becomes an overstatement of beginning inventory in Year 2. Overstated beginning inventory leads to overstated cost of goods sold in Year 2, which understates Year 2 gross profit and net income by $20,000. This is a counterbalancing error—over two years the net effect on retained earnings is zero.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1の期末棚卸資産の過大計上は、Year 2の期首棚卸資産の過大計上となります。期首棚卸資産が過大計上されると、Year 2の売上原価が過大計上され、その結果Year 2の売上総利益と当期純利益が$20,000過少計上されます。これは自動修正される誤謬（相殺誤謬）であり、2年間を通じた利益剰余金への純影響額はゼロです。
